title: Is Peanut-butter Gluten Free? | BiteRight
description: Public gluten safety analysis for Peanut-butter. See major risks, safer alternatives, and what to ask before ordering.
heading: Is Peanut-butter gluten free?
intro: This public analysis report explains the biggest gluten risks in Peanut-butter and how to order more safely.

verdict.summary: While pure peanut butter is typically gluten-free, cross-contamination during processing or the addition of gluten-containing ingredients can pose a risk for those with coeliac disease. It's important to verify the source and preparation methods.

disclaimer: This guidance is informational only. Always verify ingredients and preparation with the restaurant.

sections.title: Quick answer
sections.body: Peanut-butter can vary by recipe, ingredients, and cross-contact controls in the kitchen.

sections.title: Common gluten risks
sections.body: Watch for hidden sources like soy sauce, malt flavoring, marinades, thickeners, and shared fryers.

sections.title: How BiteRight helps
sections.body: Scan menus and ingredient labels with BiteRight to get a localized gluten-risk breakdown in seconds.

risk: Added flavorings or ingredients (e.g., malt extract)

waiter_script.preview: Can you confirm if the peanut butter is processed in a gluten-free facility?

faq.question: Can BiteRight confirm if Peanut-butter is gluten free?
faq.answer: BiteRight highlights likely gluten risks based on ingredients and preparation. Always confirm with the kitchen if you have coeliac disease.

faq.question: What should I ask a restaurant?
faq.answer: Ask about shared equipment, sauces, marinades, and whether the kitchen has a dedicated gluten-free prep area.
